page: Microsoft Azure E4ads_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Eadv6 server family. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, this instance features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The hardware configuration includes a 236 GB local storage disk and supports Premium Storage. The CPU architecture is x86_64, utilizing Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization and supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AES cryptography, and SHA extensions.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core or memory-intensive performance. The single-core CPU performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng single-core results placing in the top tier (top 10%) and Geekbench single-core scores ranking in the strong category (top 25%). However, multi-core execution drops to average levels, and memory bandwidth presents a notable bottleneck. Read-only memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks falls into the weak category (bottom 25%), though write bandwidth remains average. Similarly, complex tasks like software compilation and database operations register as weak, and medium-sized LLM inference (7B model) performs poorly, indicating this server is not optimized for heavy parallel processing or high-throughput memory operations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E4ads_v6 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for workloads that require a larger memory workspace without the need for high-core-count licensing costs. The inclusion of 236 GB of local storage adds value for temporary data caching. Given its high single-core performance but constrained memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling, this server is best suited for memory-bound workloads with moderate parallelization needs, such as caching layers, lightweight database hosting, text processing, and general-purpose web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ads_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eadv6 family, featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it includes 236 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks show exceptional single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance in the top 10% and strong Geekbench single-core scores. However, the instance exhibits tradeoffs, particularly in memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling, where read-only bandwidth and database operations fall into the bottom 25%. Qualitatively, it provides high memory density per vCPU, making it cost-efficient for memory-heavy tasks that do not require massive parallel compute. It is well-suited for caching layers, text processing, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ads_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU. It offers top-tier single-core performance but faces memory bandwidth tradeoffs.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ads_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC CPU, offering strong single-core speed with local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring strong single-core performance, dedicated AMD EPYC processing, and integrated local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with a 3.7 GHz clock speed.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and 236 GB of local storage.
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks.
Exhibits weak read-only memory bandwidth and database operation speeds.
Ideal for memory-bound workloads, caching layers, and text processing.
